context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct npm sync command with the exact project directory path, sets the bash timeout to 360000ms or more, and documents the destructive and browser-visible nature of the sync operation.

checklist.name: Correct npm command
checklist.description: sync.sh uses 'npm run sync' (not 'npm start', 'node index.js', or any other invocation)

checklist.name: Timeout set correctly
checklist.description: sync.sh or its documentation mentions a timeout of at least 360000ms (6 minutes), either as a comment or as a bash timeout flag

checklist.name: Destructive nature documented
checklist.description: At least one output file explicitly states that the sync deletes or replaces all existing Reclaim travel timezone entries before adding new ones

checklist.name: Browser opens visibly
checklist.description: At least one output file notes that the browser opens visibly (not headless), so the user can observe the process

checklist.name: Runtime expectation
checklist.description: At least one output file mentions that the operation takes approximately 1-2 minutes (or references minutes of expected runtime)

checklist.name: Login session persistence
checklist.description: README.md or run-notes.txt mentions that login sessions are stored in the .auth/ directory across runs

checklist.name: Login wait behavior
checklist.description: At least one output file mentions that if login is required, the script will pause and wait (up to 5 minutes) for manual login

checklist.name: cd before npm command
checklist.description: sync.sh uses 'cd' into the project directory before or as part of running the npm command (not just referencing the path in a comment)
